blocks.content: Shirone's team gathered at the park for the first time in a while to discuss their plans for the vacation.
If the second half of the final year was going to be more intense than the first, it was best to start preparing strategies now.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Last year, I trained alone, but I found out that some students formed teams. So, I was thinking, why don't we form a group and do a training camp together?
Amy, who had experienced the final year, suggested this, and the friends were considering it positively.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Of course, having a team is advantageous for tasks like capturing high ground or combat. But you know, only ten people will pass. Even if we form a team, we'll have to compete against each other to be in the final ten. So, how much information should we share?

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Of course, having a team is advantageous for tasks like capturing high ground or combat. But you know, only ten people will pass. Even if we form a team, we'll have to compete against each other to be in the final ten. So, how much information should we share?
If you had a trump card, it was natural to want to keep it until the final exam.
It was a problem that needed to be addressed clearly, as it was almost a miracle for all five members to pass.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I thought the same until last year. But we don't have to use the entire vacation. We can gather for basic training first, and then each of us can research our special moves individually.
Amy argued that building teamwork might increase their chances of passing more than researching special moves alone.
The reason was that the current controller of the final year was Fermi, and all of Shirone's group had been marked by him.
Since they couldn't predict what might happen on the day of the final exam, sticking together was the only way to reduce variables.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: I think Amy is right.
Nade was all for it.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: He didn't care much about the final exam, but training with friends was far more enjoyable than enduring a few months at his dreadful home.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: How about this? We do a short-term training camp for about a month, using the final year's system. We set daily goals and score ourselves.
Iruki nodded in agreement.

blocks.speaker: Iruki
blocks.content: Maintaining the final year's routine is a good idea. It will help us achieve more in a short time, and we can use the remaining time to train our special moves individually.
While various opinions were being presented, Shirone remained silent, just taking in the faces of his friends.
Nothing had been decided yet, but he felt a strange sensation.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Huh? Maya, are you sick?
Shirone asked in surprise, looking at Maya last.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Shirone.
Then, as if she had just regained her memory, she suddenly looked up and turned to Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I love you.
A silence fell over the park bench.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Are you okay, Maya? Should we take you to the infirmary?
Maya quickly shook her head.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I don't think I could muster the courage a second time, so I wanted to say it in front of everyone. I'm sorry, Shirone. But I've come to love you. Please accept my feelings.
Maya took out a letter from her pocket. It smelled of flowers.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: Will you read it?
Handing the letter to Shirone, she avoided everyone's gaze and stood up from the bench.

blocks.speaker: Maya
blocks.content: I'll hear your answer then.
Leaving those mysterious words, Maya walked away.

blocks.speaker: Nade
blocks.content: Hey, what are you going to do? Do you have someone in mind?
Shirone held the letter and looked into the distance.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: I'm kidding. Why so serious? You knew Maya liked you.
Amy smiled and lightly patted his shoulder.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Go to your room and read it alone. Read it two or three times. Then do what you want. Maya is a good girl. I'll go now. See you tomorrow. Bye.
Amy left without giving Shirone a chance to respond.

blocks.speaker: Amy
blocks.content: Her face was calm, but her voice was angry, and her actions were awkward.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: I guess I have to make a decision now.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Maybe Maya's confession was a good thing.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Before discussing grand issues like Heaven or humanity, there were things he needed to sort out first.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Nade, Iruki. I need a favor.
The next afternoon.
Shirone entered the principal's office.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: You've come, Shirone.
Siena didn't look well.
Having heard all about the existence and dangers of Heaven, the conspirators involved in this incident, and the people connected to it, she couldn't help but be emotional.

blocks.speaker: Siena
blocks.content: You must have had a hard time alone. Don't worry too much now. The teachers will protect you.
As a bishop of the Karsis Monastery, Etella wouldn't hesitate to act in the face of humanity's survival, but Siena's decision was solely for Shirone.
In a global-scale incident, there was no room for Shirone's life, emotions, or dignity. Siena chose to go to Heaven with the sole intention of protecting her student.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Teacher...
Had he unknowingly felt sorrow?
The days of nightmares, fearing his life would be destroyed by sudden events and worrying he might be executed by the National Intelligence Service, flashed before his eyes.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: You're here? I called you to share all the information as per the contract. Today, we open Istas and meet Miro. We can't talk while in deep meditation, but it's something we must confirm before going to Heaven.
Gaold's voice was excited.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It would be his first reunion with Miro in 20 years. He also knew it could be the last day he saw her.
But is it okay to go like this? There might still be agents around.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hehe, it's fine. We've come this far, it doesn't matter.
Gaold confidently left the room.
As the most powerful mage in Tormia acted as if there was no problem, the others followed without complaint.
Crossing the barricade of Istas, they saw ninety-eight warehouses scattered like dismantled blocks.
They were in the highest level of separation and had stopped functioning.
Gaold opened the cover of Istas' control panel.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Nothing has changed here.
As he fumbled, unsure of what to touch first, ten National Intelligence Service agents emerged from behind the building.

blocks.speaker: National Intelligence Service Agent
blocks.content: Mikael Gaold, you are under arrest for treason under the Kingdom Security Act.
Shirone quickly assumed a combat stance.
Realizing the feared situation had become a reality, he understood how foolish it was to follow Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Even if he was a first-class grand mage, he had overlooked the fact that Gaold was fundamentally insane.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hmm, how was this operated? Hey, was it the red button?
A cold voice came from behind the agents.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: It's the blue button, you idiot.
The agents parted, and a man walked out.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Oh, right, it was blue. Getting old makes me forgetful.
Ignoring Gaold's words, the man walked over to Alpheas.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: How have you been, Principal?
To Shirone, it looked like he moved with perfect uniform motion.

blocks.speaker: Alpheas
blocks.content: Yes. It's been a while, Sein.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Sein?
He was one of the founding members of the Paranormal Psychic Science Research Society that Gaold had mentioned.
He was also the one who created Istas' master equation.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: Shirone, use your synesthesia to examine them.
Following Etella's instruction, Shirone sensed that Sein's spirit zone was split into tendrils, connecting with all the National Intelligence Service agents.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Mind control?
Yes. And it's top-level mind control.

blocks.speaker: Etella
blocks.content: If they came from the National Intelligence Service, the agents' combat abilities would be above average. Additionally, due to their professional nature, they would have undergone resistance training against mental attacks.
A person who could successfully mind control such individuals with a 100% success rate.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Arin had mentioned that achieving this would require concentration equivalent to 20 times the mental strength of the opponent.
Istas started operating with a hum.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Sein is a savant and a mental mage. He currently operates under the alias Snake in the Black Line. Despite appearances, he's a triple-A rated criminal designated by the Red Line. You might want to get his autograph.
Gaold, who had taken his hand off the blue button, continued to look at the control panel and introduced Sein to Shirone. This was also part of the contract.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: Black Line?
The Black Line was a group that denied the taboos of magic and practiced magic based solely on personal beliefs.
It was on a different level from simply killing or tormenting people.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: If you were to name the strongest mental-type mages in the Black Line, you'd mention two people. One is Guitar Man of the Magic Seven, and the other is Snake. He'll be giving orders in our team. Of course, I'm the commander, but still.
Sein scoffed.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: You're still full of nonsense. Did playing house at the association make you a real fool? The only reason someone like you can pretend to be a commander is because my orders are precise.

blocks.speaker: Shirone
blocks.content: He has a personality similar to Iruki...
Shirone saw Iruki's arrogance in Sein.
Living with a calculation ability that transcended humans, it seemed natural for his self-confidence to peak like that.
